claims.claim: When battery temperature exceeds 100°C due to a short circuit, PTC in a high resistance state protects individual batteries from the influence of overcurrent.
claims.quote: when the battery's temperature exceeds 100 ◦C due to a short circuit, PTC in a high resistance state protects individual batteries from the influence of overcurrent.

claims.claim: The P3BT-523 composite electrode hardly discharged after exposure to 150°C for 5 minutes, indicating that PTC could suppress internal short circuits.
claims.quote: The P3BT-523 composite electrode hardly discharged after exposure to 150 ◦C for 5 min, thus indicating that PTC could suppress internal short circuits.

claims.claim: A failure of PTC in a battery pack may result in ignition and thermal runaway, as confirmed by evidence that a PTC in a 14-cell series package was ignited.
claims.quote: A failure of PTC may result in ignition and TR, as confirmed by evidence that a PTC in a 14-cell series package was ignited.

claims.claim: Both K2 18650E and LG M36 CID types could be activated at 100°C, with activation pressures of 1.781 ± 0.355 MPa and 1.799 ± 0.284 MPa, respectively.
claims.quote: both types of CID could be activated at 100 ◦C, with activation pressures of 1.781 ± 0.355 and 1.799 ± 0.284 MPa, respectively.

claims.claim: High-voltage (>50 V) CID could generate arc under overcharge conditions, and sparks and heat generated by the arc will ignite combustible gas and the battery electrolyte, resulting in thermal runaway.
claims.quote: high-voltage (>50 V) CID could generate arc under overcharge conditions, and contact surface might be erosion or ablation by high current density. Sparks and heat generated by the arc will ignite combustible gas and the battery electrolyte, which result in TR in the battery

claims.claim: A single large (>100 Ahr) lithium-ion cell at 3.5 Vdc, when short circuited, can reach temperatures exceeding 200 °F (93 °C) within a few seconds.
claims.quote: A single large (>100 Ahr) lithium-ion cell at 3.5 Vdc, short circuited, can reach temperatures exceeding 200 °F (93 °C) within a few seconds.

claims.claim: The potential for ignition during a thermal runaway event is reduced at 30% SOC compared with a higher-SOC cell, but an off-gassing lithium-ion cell produces a large quantity of toxic gases even at 30% SOC.
claims.quote: The potential for ignition during a thermal runaway event is reduced at 30% SOC compared with a higher-SOC cell. An off-gassing lithium-ion cell produces a large quantity of toxic gases even at 30% SOC.

claims.claim: Short-circuit events in lithium-ion cells can produce 20–30 C discharge current instantaneously for a very short duration (less than 1 second) and 8–10 C sustained discharge current.
claims.quote: testing data for various lithium-ion cell manufacturers have shown that short-circuit events can produce 20–30 C discharge current instantaneously for a very short duration (<1 second) and 8–10 C sustained discharge current.

claims.claim: Most industry-accepted DC arc flash incident energy calculation methods for lithium-ion battery packs yield conservative results that are magnitudes higher than actual values seen in physical tests.
claims.quote: most industry-accepted methods yield conservative results, which in many cases are magnitudes higher than actual values seen in physical tests.

claims.claim: Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Specification 305 requires a minimum of 500 ohms/volt AC and 100 ohms/volt DC isolation between the chassis ground and high-voltage conductors, which equates to a minimum isolation of 200 kilohms on a 400-Vdc system.
claims.quote: Federal Motor Vehicle Safety Specification 305 requires a minimum of 500 ohms/volt ac and 100 ohms/volt dc isolation between the chassis ground and high-voltage conductors for customer and service worker safety on vehicles. This equates to a minimum isolation of 200 kilohms on a 400-Vdc system.

claims.claim: The thermosensitive microcapsule (TM) fire extinguishing agent reduces the peak thermal runaway temperature of a single-cell LIB by 14.0 °C and decreases the heating rate by 0.17 °C/s compared to no extinguishing agent.
claims.quote: the peak TR temperature of a single-cell LIB is reduced by 14.0 ◦C, and the heating rate is decreased by 0.17 ◦C/s

claims.claim: When the LIB temperature reaches 120 °C, the MUF wall material of the TM ruptures and releases the encapsulated core fire extinguishing materials.
claims.quote: When the temperature of LIBs exceeds 120 ◦C, the core material of the TM fire extinguishing agent coated on the surface of the LIBs is automatically released, effectively reducing the temperature and mitigating TR risks.

claims.claim: The TM displays a broad endothermic peak ranging from 51 to 119.0 °C with a maximum peak at 90.8 °C, and upon exceeding 119 °C the structural integrity of the wall material is compromised, resulting in release of the core material.
claims.quote: The TM displays a broad endothermic peak ranging from 51 to 119.0 ◦C, with a maximum peak at 90.8 ◦C...Upon exceeding a temperature of 119 ◦C, the structural integrity of the wall material is compromised, resulting in the subsequent release of the core material.

claims.claim: In the dual-LIB module test with TM applied, cell body 1 reached a maximum temperature 18.0 °C lower than the highest temperature under natural cooling, and cell body 2 reached a peak of only 63 °C, which is 6.0 °C lower than without suppression.
claims.quote: This temperature is 18.0 ◦C lower than the highest temperature of natural cooling...Compared with the natural cooling to suppress the TR of the LIBs, the peak value is reduced by 6.0 ◦C

claims.claim: The Br element in 2-BTP captures free radicals within the combustion chain reaction, thereby disrupting the oxidation process and reducing the formation of flammable gases, but 2-BTP remains stable below 120 °C preventing premature decomposition during normal operation.
claims.quote: Free radicals within the combustion chain reaction can be effectively captured by the Br element in 2-BTP, thereby disrupting the oxidation process and ultimately reducing the formation of flammable gases...2-BTP remains stable below 120 ◦C, preventing premature decomposition during normal operation.
